title: Pharmaceutical Regulatory Document Automation for FDA Submissions
metaTitle: FDA Pharmaceutical Document Automation | GridPull AI
metaDescription: Automate extraction of data from FDA pharmaceutical regulatory documents into structured Excel spreadsheets. OCR-powered processing for compliance workflows.
h1: Pharmaceutical Regulatory Document Automation for FDA Submissions

hero.headline: Extract Data from FDA Regulatory Documents into Structured Spreadsheets
hero.subheadline: Convert pharmaceutical compliance documents, FDA forms, and regulatory reports into organized Excel files with AI-powered field extraction

summary: GridPull converts FDA regulatory documents, pharmaceutical compliance forms, and submission paperwork into structured Excel spreadsheets using AI-powered field extraction. Process digital PDFs, scanned documents, and images of regulatory forms to create organized data files for compliance tracking and submission preparation.
whoItsFor: Regulatory affairs specialists managing FDA submission documents
Contract research organizations processing clinical trial paperwork
whenThisIsRelevant: Processing FDA Form 356h and other submission forms
Converting scanned regulatory correspondence to searchable data
Organizing clinical trial documentation for database entry
supportedInputs: Digital PDF FDA forms and regulatory documents
expectedOutputs: Excel files with extracted regulatory data fields
CSV exports for compliance database import
commonChallenges: Manual data entry from lengthy FDA submission forms creates bottlenecks
Scanned regulatory documents require time-consuming transcription
Multiple document formats make standardized data extraction difficult
Compliance deadlines pressure teams to process documents quickly
howItWorksSteps: Upload FDA forms, regulatory documents, or compliance paperwork
AI identifies and extracts relevant data fields like drug names, submission numbers, dates
Review extracted data and customize fields for your compliance workflow
Export to Excel or CSV for regulatory database import
whyPdfExcelAiFits: OCR technology processes both digital and scanned regulatory documents
Batch processing handles multiple FDA forms simultaneously
Custom field selection adapts to different regulatory document types
Pipeline automation streamlines recurring compliance document workflows
limitations: Complex multi-page regulatory tables may require manual review
Handwritten annotations on forms have limited recognition accuracy
Document quality affects extraction precision on older scanned files

faq.question: Can this process FDA Form 356h submission documents?
faq.answer: Yes, GridPull can extract data from FDA Form 356h and other standard regulatory forms, though complex nested sections may need review for accuracy.

faq.question: Does this work with scanned pharmaceutical compliance documents?
faq.answer: Yes, OCR technology processes scanned regulatory paperwork, with accuracy depending on scan quality and document clarity.

faq.question: How accurate is data extraction from regulatory documents?
faq.answer: GridPull achieves 99%+ accuracy on clear documents, but regulatory forms with complex layouts or poor scan quality may require manual verification.

faq.question: Can I process multiple FDA submission documents at once?
faq.answer: Yes, batch processing allows you to upload and convert multiple regulatory documents simultaneously into organized spreadsheet formats.

trustSignals: Files encrypted and automatically deleted after processing
Free tier available for testing with your documents
Plans starting at $49/month for regular processing needs
exampleUseCases: Converting FDA correspondence into trackable compliance data
Processing clinical trial documentation for regulatory databases
Extracting submission numbers and dates from regulatory forms
Organizing pharmaceutical inspection reports into searchable spreadsheets
